semanticTypesRegistry.abstract: This annex defines the canonical Type Registry for the E.L.I.A. type system. It enumerates all primitive, block, derived, and stream carrier...

semanticClassification.abstract: Semantic Types define what data means and what is admissible to do with it within a domain.

explanation.abstract: Explanation is a first-class semantic construct responsible for making *meaning explicit, inspectable, and traceable* for both humans and machines.

definition.abstract: Definition is a first-class semantic construct that *designates meaning and obligations* for a domain concept.

dataSurrogate.abstract: A Data Surrogate is a first-class data artefact that becomes *semantically visible* by attaching Explanation mechanics (and optionally...

behavioralSurrogate.abstract: A Behavioral Surrogate is a behavioral construct that becomes *semantically inspectable and governable* by attaching Explanation mechanics...

inline.abstract: inline is a directive for compact declarative specification of standardized operations inside an action body.

data_interface.abstract: A Data Interface is a first-class semantic construct that projects defined meaning onto concrete data surfaces.

seq.H2: Semantic Interface — the mechanism for binding meanings

semantic_interface.abstract: A Semantic Interface is a first-class semantic construct that *projects defined meaning into admissible behavioral interaction*.

delegate.abstract: Delegate is a first-class semantic construct that establishes an explicit trust and execution boundary between E.L.I.A.

domain.abstract: binds abstract meaning to real-world responsibility;

semanticBoundary.abstract: Explicit semantic boundaries define where meaning is authoritative, where it may be relied upon, and where it is explicitly prohibited from emerging.

enforcement.abstract: Enforcement in E.L.I.A. is a first-class architectural construct, not an implementation technique, runtime policy, or auxiliary validation layer.

seq.H2: Semantic Layer in the Language Model

semanticModel.abstract: Semantic model in E.L.I.A. is declarative and deterministic (consists of finite set of primitives).

domainAuthority.abstract: Before introducing purpose and structure, it is important to clarify why a domain may legitimately contain semantic interfaces.

semanticEnforcement.abstract: This annex defines the computational, classificatory, and enforcement semantics of rules.

semanticTypes.abstract: Semantic Types define what data means and what is admissible to do with it within a domain.

semanticSurrogates.abstract: Surrogates serve exclusively to preserve semantic integrity, enforce phase-wise determinism, and maintain continuity of authority across multi-step,...

non-value.abstract: Special/control primitives represent semantic control markers, not domain data.

domainDrivenFlow.abstract: Layer 1 defines intent, prohibitions, and obligations.
